slides.title: HOW THIS DECK WORKS

sections.heading: Vocabulary Anchor
sections.body: Introduce the key term, definition, and examples.

sections.heading: Core Reading / Lab
sections.body: Students engage with text or lab, building understanding.

sections.heading: Concept Mapping
sections.body: Students create a graphic organiser connecting to prior knowledge.

sections.heading: Review & Retrieval
sections.body: Quick retrieval practice with target vocabulary.

sections.heading: Extension Activity
sections.body: Apply the concept to a new scenario or problem set.

sections.heading: Small Group / Lab
sections.body: Hands-on lab or small group inquiry task.

slides.footer: Each unit = multiple cycles of 3 days  •  Assessment embedded throughout

slides.title: SCOPE & SEQUENCE — BIOLOGY 12

content.prompt: Biology 12 moves from cell structures and membrane transport into genetics, evolution, and ecosystems. Each unit pairs a key concept with classroom activities and assessment evidence.

rows: Cell structure, membrane transport, cell division
Microscopy lab, osmosis lab, mitosis model
DNA structure, replication, protein synthesis, heredity
DNA extraction lab, Punnett square problems
Energy flow, nutrient cycles, biodiversity, human impact

content.prompt: Unit 1 anchors cell biology through mitosis, osmosis, and meiosis. Students build vocabulary, connect organelle function to cell processes, and apply the ideas in lab work.

columns.title: Vocabulary Anchor
items: Organelle: a specialised structure within a cell
Identify the organelles in the cell model

left_card.label: VOCABULARY ANCHOR
left_card.content:
1. Nucleus    2. Mitochondria
3. Ribosome   4. Cell membrane
5. Endoplasmic reticulum  6. Golgi apparatus
7. Chloroplast (plant)    8. Vacuole

right_cards.label: MEMBRANE TRANSPORT
right_cards.content:
Diffusion  →  movement from high to low concentration
Osmosis  →  diffusion of water across a membrane
Active transport  →  requires energy, moves against gradient

right_cards.label: CONCEPT MAPPING
right_cards.content:
Draw and label a plant cell and an animal cell.
Mark the following on each:
  •  Nucleus   •  Cell membrane   •  Mitochondria
  •  Ribosome   •  Vacuole   •  Chloroplast (plant only)

prompts: What is the purpose of mitosis?
How many cells does meiosis produce?
Where does meiosis occur in the body?

left_card.label: CELL DIVISION REVIEW
left_card.highlight_instruction:
Highlight all MITOSIS steps in yellow.
Highlight all MEIOSIS steps in blue.
left_card.content:
1. What is the purpose of mitosis?
2. How many cells does meiosis produce?
3. Where does meiosis occur in the body?

right_cards.label: LAB ACTIVITY
right_cards.content: Onion Root Tip Lab: Use a prepared slide to observe and sketch cells in different phases of mitosis. Identify: interphase, prophase, metaphase, anaphase, telophase.

right_cards.label: ENRICHMENT
right_cards.content: Cancer Connection: Research how uncontrolled mitosis leads to tumour formation. Use the class article set to answer: What goes wrong in the cell cycle during cancer?
